title: Ephemeral gateway for remote access
abstract: A computer implemented method includes receiving (210) a time window for allowing access to an asset in a local area network. An ephemeral gateway is instantiated (220) to provide access to the asset during the time window. Time window limited access credentials are also identified (230) and are required for a first remote user to access the ephemeral gateway to gain access to the asset only during the time window.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer implemented method (200) comprising: receiving (210, 540) a time window (125) for allowing access to an asset (317) in a local area network (115); instantiating (220, 560) an ephemeral gateway (130) to provide access to the asset during the time window; and identifying (230) time w indow limited access credentials (336) required for a first remote user (120) to access the ephemeral gatew ay to gain access to the asset only during the time window. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein selecting a time window^ is performed via a privileged access calendar function. 3. The method of claim 2 comprising providing (555) the first remote user the time window limited access credentials via a calendar invite. 4. The method of claim 3 wherein the credentials are specific to the time window and are only valid during the time w indow . 5. The method of claim 2 wherein instantiating the ephemeral gateway is performed via the privileged access calendar. 6. The method of claim 1 wherein the ephemeral gateway is initiated (550) at a time prior to a beginning (545) of the time window- and removed (565) at an end of the time window. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6 and further comprising: receiving (240) the time window limited credentials from the first remote user; and granting (250) access to the asset in response to receiving the time window limited credentials within the time indow. 8. The method of claim 7 wherein granting access to the asset comprises establishing a virtual private netw ork tunnel (350) betw een the ephemeral gatew ay and the local area network. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein certificate services () are used to establish the virtual private network. 10. The method of claim 7 w herein granting access to the asset is only done in response to an IP address of the remote user being included in a list of permitted IP addresses. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-6 w herein the time window limited access credentials are stored in a cloud-based password management vault (336). 12. The method of any one of claims 1-6 wherein the ephemeral gateway comprises a jump box. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-6 wherein the local area network comprises a cloud computing network (315) provisioned for multiple remote users and wherein the ephemeral 

gateway limits the first remote user access to the asset associated with the first remote user. 14. A machine-readable storage device (603) having instructions (618) for execution by a processor (602) of a machine (600) to cause the processor to perform operations to perform a method, the operations comprising: receiving a time window for allowing access to an asset in a local area network; instantiating an ephemeral gateway to provide access to the asset during the time window; and identifying time window limited access credentials required for a first remote user to access the ephemeral gateway to gain access to the asset only during the time window. 15. The device of claim 14 wherein selecting a time window is performed via a privileged access calendar function and providing the first remote user the time window limited access credentials via a calendar invite. 16. The device of claim 14 wherein the ephemeral gateway is initiated at a time prior to a beginning of the time window and removed at an end of the time window. 17. The device of any one of claims 14-16 wherein the operation further comprise: receiving the time window limited credentials from the first remote user; and granting access to the asset in response to receiving the time window limited credentials within the time window. 18. The device of claim 17 wherein granting access to the asset comprises establishing a virtual private network tunnel between the ephemeral gateway and the local area network and wherein granting access to the asset is only done in response to an IP address of the remote user being included in a list of permitted IP addresses. 19. A device (600) comprising: a processor (602); and a memory device (603) coupled to the processor and having a program (618) stored thereon for execution by the processor to perform operations comprising: receiving a time window for allowing access to an asset in a local area network: instantiating an ephemeral gateway to provide access to the asset during the time window; and identifying time window limited access credentials required for a first remote user to access the ephemeral gateway to gain access to the asset only during the time window. 20. The device of claim 19 wherein the operation further comprise: receiving the time window limited credentials from the first remote user; and granting access to the asset in response to receiving the time window limited credentials within the time window.
